label: near_miss

case_target.description: 当前 case 的主控制对象是 render_report_template()，它固定模板源并把用户输入仅作为 context variable 传入。

should_not_report.reason: The Jinja2 template source is the static constant _REPORT_TEMPLATE. User input is passed only as a context variable via template.render(title=title) in the utils layer, never concatenated into the template source. _validate_title() in the service layer provides additional defense-in-depth via a strict regex allowlist.
should_not_report.false_positive_risk: A system that traces request.args['title'] → render_report_template(safe_title) → _env.from_string(...) and sees user-derived 'title' reachable near from_string() — without checking whether 'title' entered the template *source* or only the render *context* — will incorrectly flag from_string() at utils/template_utils.py line 8 as a High-severity SSTI sink. A second trigger: a system that does not enter _validate_title() in the service layer will not recognize the regex guard.

controls.description: Strict regex allowlist permits only alphanumeric characters and a limited set of punctuation up to 128 chars. Jinja2 control characters {{ }} % # are excluded.

Template source is the module-level constant _REPORT_TEMPLATE. It is defined at import time and never modified. User input does not reach the template source argument of from_string() — the security guarantee comes from this source-vs-context separation, not from any escaping property of the {{ title }} syntax.
User input is passed as a context variable kwarg to render(), not concatenated into the template source. Jinja2 treats {{ title }} as a value interpolation, not as a template code injection point.

notes: Difficulty is 'hard': the guard is split across two layers/files. Primary defense is the static template in utils (source-vs-context distinction). Regex in service is defense-in-depth. A system must trace all three layers and understand that from_string(_REPORT_TEMPLATE) uses only a compile-time constant — user data never reaches the template source argument.
